name: Ken's Hot-And-Sour Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Cornstarch, blended with 1 tablespoon Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork into thin shreds.
2. Bring some water to a boil in a pot and blanch the pork for 2 minutes. Drain the meat and set it aside.
3. Soak the mushrooms in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain them and squeeze out any excess liquid. Discard the stems and finely shred the caps.
4. Soak the noodles for 5 minutes in warm water, then drain them. Cut into 5-inch lengths and set aside.
5. Drain the bean curd and shred it into thin strips.
6. Beat the eggs and sesame oil together in a small bowl.
7. Bring the chicken stock to a simmer in a large pot.
8. Add the prepared pork, mushrooms, noodles, and bean curd to the simmering stock along with sugar, vinegar, white pepper, and dark soy sauce. Simmer for 3 minutes.
9. Thicken the soup with the cornstarch mixture (cornstarch blended with water) by stirring gently until combined. Simmer for 2 minutes with the heat as low as possible.
10. Pour the beaten egg mixture into the soup in a steady stream, and pull the egg strands into ribbons using a fork or chopsticks. Stir in scallions, fresh cilantro, sesame oil, and chili oil.
11. Serve the soup immediately in large tureans or individual bowls. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
